title: Resistance
excerpt: Resistance reduces incoming damage from a named source or category. When resistance applies, the protected damage is reduced before it is applied to hit points.
markdown:
# Resistance

Resistance is a damage-resolution rule that reduces damage from a named source, category, or damage type. The protection applies only to the damage the granting rule specifies.

## Rules

- Resistance always applies to something named by a feature, trait, spell, item, or rule
- When resistance applies, the protected damage is reduced before that damage is applied to hit points
- Resistance weakens damage; it does not prevent the damage event from existing
- A creature or object can resist one category of damage while remaining fully affected by other categories

## What Resistance Covers

### Damage Type Resistance
A common form of resistance reduces damage from a specific type, such as fire, cold, or bludgeoning.

### Source-Limited Resistance
Some rules reduce damage only from a narrower source, such as nonmagical weapons, environmental harm, or a particular class of effect.

## Resistance Versus Other Defenses

Resistance is not the same as [[immunity]]. Immunity prevents the named result from applying at all. Resistance still allows the damage to land, but at a reduced amount.

Resistance is also different from Armor Class, cover, and [[temporary_hp|Temporary Hit Points]]. Those rules change whether a strike connects, how hard it is to affect a target, or what pool absorbs the damage first. Resistance changes the amount of protected damage that remains to be applied.

## Scope Matters

Resistance applies only where the granting rule says it does. A creature can resist one damage type and still take normal damage from other types, or resist a specific source while remaining vulnerable to similar harm from a different source.
